channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How a township mentorship programme is shaping healthier masculinity and opportunities for boys in Cape Town
item.description: In Cape Town’s townships, the Heroes Academy NGO is running a long‑term mentorship programme that helps boys challenged by poverty, gangsterism and gender‑based violence (GBV) to develop healthier ide...

item.title: Tanzanian Leaders Commend Election Investigation Commission for Transparency and Professionalism
item.description: Former Chief Justice Ibrahim Juma together with Tanzania’s Mufti Abubakar Zubeir have publicly praised the commission set up to investigate events surrounding the October 29 elections. They both stres...

item.title: Pakistan to Host Second Round of Iran-US Talks Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan is set to host a second round of high-level talks between Iran and the United States by the end of next week, according to sources. Security and administrative preparations are und...

item.title: Funeral arrangements announced for late Nigerian editor Godwin Benson
item.description: The family of the late Godwin Benson, who was the news editor at Akwa Ibom State-owned The Pioneer newspapers, don announce say e go dey buried on 25 April for Ikot Akpa Nkuk, Ukanafun Local Governmen...

item.title: Five wounded in suspected gang shootings in Bonteheuwel
item.description: Five people, including four children, were injured in two separate shooting incidents in Bonteheuwel on Tuesday evening, in what authorities suspect to be gang-related retaliation. The South African P...

item.title: Centre-Opposition Clash on Delimitation Bill and CBSE Class 10 Results Announced

item.title: Nigerian Military Accused of Planning Secret Hearings for Detained Officers Over Alleged Coup Plot
item.description: Fresh concern has trailed reports that the Nigerian military is preparing secret, staggered hearings for officers detained over an alleged coup plot. According to multiple sources cited in the report,...

item.title: Arc Raiders Players Choose Cooperation Over Conflict in Post‑Apocalyptic Shooter

item.title: Chris Hayes Criticizes JD Vance's Recent Diplomatic Setbacks on MSNBC
item.description: On his MSNBC show, host Chris Hayes analyzed what he described as a challenging week for Vice President JD Vance, comparing it to the chaotic and often ineffective vice presidency portrayed in the HBO...

item.title: Pittsburgh's Potential Picks in the 2026 NFL Draft: Makai Lemon and Ty Simpson Highlight Prospects
item.description: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette has released its 2026 NFL mock draft, spotlighting potential selections for the Pittsburgh Steelers. The mock draft discusses top prospects such as Makai Lemon and Ty Simps...

item.title: Union Housing Ministry to Release Urban Challenge Funds from September for Infrastructure Projects
item.description: Starting September, the Union Housing and Urban Affairs Ministry, led by Manohar Lal Khattar, will release 30% of central funds to cities for projects under the ₹1 lakh crore Urban Challenge Fund (UCF...

item.title: Canadian Youth Job Market: Challenges and Government Initiatives
item.description: A recent report highlights the difficulties Canadian young people are facing in securing employment, particularly in Calgary. The news article details a concerning trend of rising unemployment rates a...

item.title: Canadian grocery suppliers introduce temporary fuel surcharges amid rising oil prices
item.description: Several Canadian food suppliers, including Sunrise Farms, CTS Foods, Maple Leaf, and Tree of Life, have implemented temporary fuel surcharges to offset rising transportation costs due to increasing gl...

item.title: Tanzania appreciates IITA's role in advancing agriculture and sustainability
item.description: Tanzania's government has expressed deep appreciation for the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)'s ongoing support in strengthening the agricultural economy and promoting environme...

item.title: Foreign press group condemns Israel for circulating AI‑altered image of slain Lebanese journalist
item.description: A foreign media association has strongly criticised the Israeli military for using an AI‑generated and edited image to discredit a Lebanese journalist, Ali Shoeib, who was killed in an Israeli airstri...

item.title: Sascoc President Addresses Challenges in South African Sports Federations

item.title: Legal Framework Needed to Sustain US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: While the recent US-Iran ceasefire of April 8, 2026, has provided temporary relief, experts caution that it is not a permanent solution. Hassan Aslam Shad argues that without a legally binding framewo...

item.title: Dar City step up preparations for BAL finals in Kigali
item.description: Dar City basketball team have officially resumed training in Dar es Salaam as they gear up for the Basketball Africa League (BAL) finals to be held in Kigali, Rwanda, from May 22 to 31. The team, repr...

item.title: CRDB Bank Group Channels 13.7 Trillion in Loans to Boost East and Central Africa Economy
